ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT IT OWED NO DUTY TO PLAINTIFF. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FURTHER DETAILS.)

Off Calendar Jun-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: INTERVENOR FIREMAN'S FUND INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA, INC.

Appearances: Michael D. Levinson of Brayton Purcell, LLP (415.898.1555) for plaintiff and opposing party; Richard Lee of Selman Breitman (415.979.2006) for intervenor and moving party Fireman's Fund Insurance Company.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on August 15, 2012 is set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. The default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on December 8, 2015 is set aside pursuant to C.C.P. 473(b) and pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default judgment on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Plaintiffs' request for a continuance is denied. Order signed. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Michele Lucas, CSR 4017 (Aiken Welch, 510.451.1580) (503/GLW)

MINI MINUTES: Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment.

APPEARANCES: Chris Hersom, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP 415/898-1555 for pltf & opposing party; no other appearances.

The Court received notice that counsel for defendant would appear to contest the tentative ruling, however no appearance was made at the hearing. The Court called counsel's office to inquire if counsel's delay was due to the BART strike/transportation issues & if he still intended to appear. However, his office could not reach him. The Court was inclined to continue the matter. Plaintiff's counsel requested however that defendant show good cause first why he did not appear/contact the Court before the motion would be heard. The Court continued the motion to 07/10/13 at 9:30am and also set an OSC for defendant's counsel to show good cause why he did not appear at the hearing or contact the Court. Plaintiff's counsel to give notice. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Valerie L. Padilla of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3081 (510/451-1580) (503/TLJ)
***After session, counsel for defendant, Jasun Molinelli, appeared indicating that he was in Dept. 606. Counsel stated that he would submit to the tentative ruling. The Court therefore vacated its prior ruling on the record continuing the motion and setting the OSC, and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Albay Construction Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty to plaintiff. Plaintiff's counsel will prepare a proposed form of order. Judge: Teri L. Jackson (503/TLJ)
